---
title: "Course Schedule"
subtitle: "10-session syllabus for Inference and Intervention"
---

## Course Structure

This course consists of 10 sessions, each corresponding to one chapter of the textbook. Sessions are grouped into four thematic parts that build on each other sequentially.

::: {.callout-box}
**Prerequisite chain:** Each chapter builds on the previous ones. The dependency chain runs Ch1 → Ch2 → Ch3 → Ch4 → Ch5 → Ch6 → Ch7 → Ch8 → Ch9 → Ch10. It is strongly recommended to follow the chapters in order.
:::

## Part I: Foundations (Sessions 1--3)

| Session | Chapter | Topics | R Packages | Preparation |
|---------|---------|--------|------------|-------------|
| 1 | [Ch 1: Introduction to Causal Analysis](chapters/ch01.qmd) | Situational assessment vs. intervention; correlation ≠ causation; confounding, reverse causation, selection bias | `dagitty`, `ggdag` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 1; install R packages |
| 2 | [Ch 2: Qualitative Causal Models](chapters/ch02.qmd) | Four node types; signed directed links; serial, diverging, converging triplets; d-separation | `dagitty`, `ggdag` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 2 |
| 3 | [Ch 3: The MNH Diagnostic Case Study](chapters/ch03.qmd) | Iterative model building; stakeholder interviews; root node identification; prioritizing recommendations | `dagitty`, `ggdag` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 3 |

## Part II: Quantitative Analysis (Sessions 4--6)

| Session | Chapter | Topics | R Packages | Preparation |
|---------|---------|--------|------------|-------------|
| 4 | [Ch 4: Quantitative Causal Models](chapters/ch04.qmd) | Probability distributions; CPTs; Bayes' rule; Causal Markov Condition; Bayesian networks | `bnlearn`, `ggplot2` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 4 |
| 5 | [Ch 5: Situational Analysis](chapters/ch05.qmd) | Marginal probabilities; belief updating; inference in serial, diverging, converging structures; explaining away | `bnlearn`, `ggplot2` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 5 |
| 6 | [Ch 6: Simpson's Paradox & Cost-Effectiveness](chapters/ch06.qmd) | Simpson's Paradox; Prosecutor's Fallacy; causal reasoning in cost-effectiveness analysis | `ggplot2` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 6 |

## Part III: Decision & Strategy (Sessions 7--9)

| Session | Chapter | Topics | R Packages | Preparation |
|---------|---------|--------|------------|-------------|
| 7 | [Ch 7: Decision Analysis & Value of Information](chapters/ch07.qmd) | Do-calculus; expected utility; decision trees; value of information; optimal intervention design | `bnlearn`, `ggplot2` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 7 |
| 8 | [Ch 8: Multi-Country Resource Allocation](chapters/ch08.qmd) | Portfolio optimization; allocation under uncertainty; country-level analysis; budget distribution | `ggplot2`, `tidyr` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 8 |
| 9 | [Ch 9: Game Theory & Strategic Interactions](chapters/ch09.qmd) | Nash equilibrium; dominant strategies; incentive design; donor-government dynamics | `ggplot2` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 9 |

## Part IV: Data-Driven Methods (Session 10)

| Session | Chapter | Topics | R Packages | Preparation |
|---------|---------|--------|------------|-------------|
| 10 | [Ch 10: Structure Learning from Observational Data](chapters/ch10.qmd) | Markov equivalence; PC algorithm; hill-climbing; instrumental variables; causal regression | `bnlearn`, `dagitty`, `ggdag`, `ivreg` | Read Ryall & Bramson Ch 10 |

## Chapter Dependency Chain

```
Ch 1 (Introduction)
 └─→ Ch 2 (Qualitative DAGs)
      └─→ Ch 3 (Interview Case Study)
           └─→ Ch 4 (Quantitative / CPTs / Bayes)
                └─→ Ch 5 (Inference / Belief Updating)
                     └─→ Ch 6 (Simpson's Paradox)
                          └─→ Ch 7 (Decision Analysis / do-calculus)
                               └─→ Ch 8 (Resource Allocation)
                                    └─→ Ch 9 (Game Theory)
                                         └─→ Ch 10 (Structure Learning)
```

## Session Format

Each session follows a consistent structure:

1. **Theory presentation** (lecture slides) — Core concepts from the textbook, adapted to MNH contexts
2. **MNH application** — Real-world examples applying the theory to maternal and newborn health programs
3. **R code workshop** — Hands-on coding exercises using the chapter's R packages
4. **Key takeaways and discussion** — Summary and preview of the next session

## Textbook

Ryall, M. D., & Bramson, A. (2014). *Inference and Intervention: Causal Models for Business Analysis*. Routledge.

All textbook examples have been adapted from business contexts to maternal and newborn health program analysis.
